Okay, welcome to the second bonus close. This is called the Minimum Viable Commitment Quickest Valuable Win Challenge. And the reason we have this is everyone loves a good challenge. Like you're guaranteed in the last session, it really invites that resourcefulness. It sets the goalposts for them to hit and then start planning how to do that. So it orients them in the process of achieving that goal, which presupposes the decision to act. You know, they can't imagine themselves, you know, taking on that challenge and doing the things associated with it if they hadn't first decided to act. So the other note here is the more time and energy that they spend in the future as an owner of your product, the more likely they are to buy it to satisfy the reality they've already constructed and inhabited in their minds. With that said, if there's an aspect of your product or a way for your prospect to achieve a quick, valuable win that on its own would be well worth the investment, using the challenge close is a really good way to end your sales page. So to do this, we just have to revisit two concepts one last time. There's the Minimum Viable Commitment. So the simplest form of this is try this in 14 days, do these specific actions. If that doesn't work out, then we could hit undo. So this is very similar to your golden guarantee earlier. You could revisit that. It's just really making it as minimum as possible. The second one is the quickest, most valuable win. So this is an aspect or an action they're going to be taking within the user experience of your product that could produce the most valuable win. If this is a course, it's not necessarily the first lesson or the first module. When we launched 10x launches, the quickest, most valuable win was the sales theme plates that didn't come until modules six and seven. Those were the ones that they could use. They could fire off a sales email within 25 minutes and get an ROI on the entire investment. So look through every aspect of your product. Look through all modules. Look at all components and try to identify the component that will give them the quickest, most viable win. And then you want to stack those two concepts on top of each other in a way that looks like this. Headline, take the X challenge. Then you'll remind them of the long-term wins and ultimate outcomes of your product. And then challenge them to take the MVC, the minimum viable commitment, and the resulting quickest, viable win that they'll be striving for. So to bring it back to the 10x launches example, take the 10x ROI challenge. Sure, in 10x launches, you're going to be introduced to the coaching the conversion method, which will help you permanently divorce yourself from the version of copyrighting you lose sleep over and instead initiate you into a brand new paradigm, one that's more profitable, easier to execute, and falls completely in line with your existing skills and talents as a coach or educator. So that's looking at the long haul, the ultimate promise. But unlike other programs, I don't want you to wait eight weeks before kinda maybe sorta seeing a result. Here's the minimum viable commitment. Enroll right now, watch a few short lessons, and make plans to fire off your first theme plate within the next day or two. So very specific, immediate instructions. We're not asking them to watch the whole program. We're not asking them to use every component of the product. We're literally asking for about an hour total of their time as a minimum viable commitment. If you don't feel like you're well on your way to 10xing your investment, just let us know and we'll fire back your investment so that you'll only have to scrape together another 8 or 10k to get someone to do it for you. Yes, I'm ready to take the 10x challenge. So that was the button that just really ties in together the challenge aspect. So this has all those components together. We started with the grand big outcomes that the whole course is gonna produce, and then we narrowed it down. You know, minimum viable commitment. Enroll right now, watch a few short lessons, and the action firing off that theme plate, and then the most valuable win. We want them to be 10xing their investment based on that. And then we challenge them to do it. So the goal posts are very simple. They know the game they're playing. They can invite resourcefulness to do it. And most importantly, they can envision themselves in this process in a very exciting, rewarding way that presupposes the decision of having purchased the program. So that's it. That is the final close I wanted to revisit with you. Once again, if you want to play around with even more closes that don't necessarily blend well on the sales page, but can inspire other angles you might want to incorporate here, do look at module 7 of 10x launches if you have access. Otherwise, this is all you need to coach the conversion on your sales page. I am so grateful that you went through this program, went through the writing workshops, and I look forward to connecting with you further. Take care.
